fields.name: mass scale#scales the mass of each rigid body.  If you leave this field as 0, then it will be calculated from the total mass below.

mass#override total mass of all rigid bodies.  Note that this will NOT be the mass of the object if not all rigid bodies are present (for example if you are using permutations)
If you set a mass scale above, this field is unused.
low freq. deactivation scale#0 is default (1). LESS than 1 deactivates less aggressively. GREATER than 1 is more agressive.
high freq. deactivation scale#0 is default (1). LESS than 1 deactivates less aggressively. GREATER than 1 is more agressive.
custom shape radius!#0 defaults to .016.  This field is intentionally hidden because we should only alter this for very special situations.  Lower number == lower performance
maximum penetration depth scale#0 is default (1). for objects that are prone to falling through the world we can reduce this number at the cost of performance
minimum force#0 defaults to maximum force.  In general you should leave this alone unless working on ragdolls or something like them
minimum force#0 defaults to maximum force.  In general you should leave this alone unless working on ragdolls or something like them
proportion recover vel#fraction of recover velocity used.  0 defaults to 1
consant recover vel#velocity used to recover when errors happen.  in degress per second
brittle timer:seconds#objects in this phantom voulume will be set to brittle collision damage for this amount of time.
marker name#you don't need this if you're just generating effects.  If empty it defaults to the up of the object
alignment marker name#you don't need this if you're just generating effects.  If empty it defaults to "marker name"
Linear Motion
fields.definition:
0 - means do nothing
CENTER: motion towards marker position 
AXIS: motion towards marker axis, such that object is on the axis
DIRECTION: motion along marker direction

fields.name: hookes law e#0 if you don't want this to behave like spring.  1 is a good starting point if you do.
linear dead radius#radius from linear motion origin in which acceleration is dead.
Angular Motion
fields.definition:
0 - means do nothing
ALIGNMENT: algin objects in the phantom with the marker
SPIN: spin the object about the marker axis

fields.name: alignment hookes law e#0 if you don't want this to behave like spring.  1 is a good starting point if you do.
node a material#if you don't fill this out we will pluck the material from the first primitive, of the first rigid body attached to node a
node b material#if you don't fill this out we will pluck the material from the first primitive, of the first rigid body attached to node b, if node b is none we use whatever material a has
friction#0 is the default (takes what it was set in max) anything else overrides that value
collision group override
fields.definition: this value overrides the default group.  It also overrides values specified in the materials block of this tag.

fields.name: inertia tensor scales
fields.definition: if you want to set he scale uniforml just set "inertia tensor scale".  If you want to set the values seperately per axis you can set "inertia tensor scale x\y\z"

fields.name: inertia tensor scale x#0.0 defaults to 1.0
inertia tensor scale y#0.0 defaults to 1.0
inertia tensor scale z#0.0 defaults to 1.0
linear damping#this goes from 0-10 (10 is really, really high)
angular damping#this goes from 0-10 (10 is really, really high)
water physics bounding box
fields.definition: if any of these fields are non-zero, this overrides the bounding box used for water physics

fields.name: water physics x0#x0 value of the water physics aabb
water physics x1#x1 value of the water physics aabb
water physics y0#y0 value of the water physics aabb
water physics y1#y1 value of the water physics aabb
water physics z0#z0 value of the water physics aabb
water physics z1#z1 value of the water physics aabb
bounding sphere pad#the bounding sphere for this rigid body will be outset by this much
collision group override
fields.definition: this value overrides the default collision group.  It is overridden by any values specified rigid bodies block that overlap.

options: is physical child#this constraint will only be created when this object is a child of another physical object (turrets on vehicles for example)
is rigid#this constraint makes the edge rigid until it is loosened by damage
disable effects#this constraint will not generate impact effects
not created automatically#this flag is used for special systems that need to create constraints dynamically
has no phantom power version#don't check this flag without talking to eamon
infinite intertia tensor#rigid body will never have angular velocity
can use mopps#this flag was invented for the behemoth and is dangerous to use anywhere else
